validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Protocol concepts require understanding what the Internet is and how it operates.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Layering organizes protocols into a stack; requires protocol fundamentals first.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Edge devices and access networks are part of the Internet's physical structure.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Physical media types connect edge devices; taught after edge topology.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Packet switching, circuit switching, and ISP hierarchy are core Internet architecture.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Delay decomposition and packet loss arise from store-and-forward switching in the core.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Understanding which layer contributes which delay component requires layering knowledge.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: The physical layer is defined within the layered architecture.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Link-layer framing and error detection sit directly above physical transmission.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Network-layer forwarding and routing rely on link-layer delivery between hops.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Security threats target protocol weaknesses; requires protocol understanding.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: The application layer is the top of the protocol stack; layering context is essential.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Client-server architecture and socket API are application-layer fundamentals.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Applications choose transport services; understanding both layers is needed.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Application protocols are designed around available transport capabilities.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Protocol message exchange assumes the client-server and socket model.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: HTTP is the primary application-layer protocol case study.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Cookies add state management to the stateless HTTP protocol.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Caching and conditional GET are HTTP-level optimization mechanisms.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: DNS is a critical application-layer infrastructure service.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: P2P is an alternative application architecture to client-server.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: DASH and adaptive streaming are built on HTTP.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: CDNs use DNS-based redirection for server selection.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: CDNs are primarily motivated by large-scale video delivery.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: The transport layer is defined within the protocol stack.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Transport services are built on top of network-layer best-effort delivery.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Application-side transport requirements motivate transport-layer internals.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Mux/demux is the first transport-layer mechanism taught.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: UDP is a minimal transport using connectionless multiplexing and checksums.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Reliable transfer (rdt1-3) is the theoretical foundation of transport reliability.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: GBN and SR are pipelined extensions of stop-and-wait reliable transfer.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: TCP segment fields (ports, seq/ack numbers) build on mux/demux concepts.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: RTT estimation uses TCP sequence numbers and acknowledgment timing.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: TCP's reliability mechanism is a practical realization of pipelined ARQ.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: TCP reliable transfer depends on segment fields like sequence numbers and flags.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Three-way handshake and teardown use TCP flags defined in the segment structure.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Congestion control adjusts the sending rate of TCP's reliable transfer mechanism.

validated_edges.subject: cn
validated_edges.rationale: Congestion manifests as increased delay and packet loss in the network.
